newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. Fabrinet's record fiscal Q3 2026 results (revenue +39.3% YoY, record telecom of $628.3M, datacom/DCI strength in 400ZR/800ZR) were reported May 4 and predate the prior case. The optics/1.6T manufacturing thesis is unchanged.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamentals since June 9; the -1.2% drift is noise. FN remains peripheral to the core passive/power bottleneck stack on this list, and the prior small up-move (de-crowding flush) is already reflected. Optics-manufacturing thesis intact but not central here.
verdicts.bull: 1.6T/Blackwell transceiver and DCI manufacturing leverage with record telecom revenue.
verdicts.neutral: Strong but well-understood optical contract manufacturer; peripheral to passives/power lens.
verdicts.bear: Customer-concentration and AI-capex cyclicality; not a true passives bottleneck.

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing genuinely new since June 9. FN is a widely-owned, well-covered optical AI beneficiary, so the latent/under-covered premise is weak and it stays deep in the list. Prior reversal on the washout is already worked in; flat price move adds nothing.
verdicts.bull: Optical-equipment exposure to AI/datacom with record fiscal Q3.
verdicts.neutral: Latent-alpha premise undercut by broad ownership and sell-side coverage.
verdicts.bear: Crowded name; little hidden asymmetry left.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 15: scale-out optics thesis (1.6T, CPO-adjacent, Blackwell transceivers) intact, but no new fundamental evidence since June 9 to leapfrog names with more direct bottleneck criticality. May Q3 print predates the case.
verdicts.bull: Scale-out bandwidth and high-speed optics assembly leader; record results.
verdicts.neutral: Execution-led, measured-YTD optics play in the master list.
verdicts.bear: Assembly margins and pricing pressure as volumes scale.
